Fix is one-line column swap (version -> name) in engine/tools/check_target.py:114, outside P5-01a's scope_lock. Tracked at Issue #9.
working_on: Phase 5 Epistemology core seed (P5-01a) — first routine-mode session against T-PHASE-5. Author 0011_seed_epistemology_part1.sql with ~25-30 foundational concept nodes (knowledge, belief, truth, justification, JTB, Gettier and four standard responses, theories of justification, foundationalism/coherentism/infinitism) plus within-domain pedagogical_prerequisite edges. Apply via Supabase MCP; verify with validate.py.
declared_scope: Author product/seed-graph/migrations/0011_seed_epistemology_part1.sql with foundational epistemology concepts at the granularity principle (~25-30 nodes; cf. seed-chunked-authoring.md worked example) and within-domain pedagogical_prerequisite edges only. Append per-session narrative entry to product/seed-graph/migrations/ROUTING.md. Apply migration via Supabase MCP apply_migration. Run engine/tools/validate.py --validate-only with the venv Python so the live graph audit engages. Confidence_level composition: INTERPRETED >= 70%, EXTRACTED rare, SYNTHETIC <= 20% per phase_5.md T2-B. PREDICATE_MANIFEST.md not edited (only pedagogical_prerequisite used). Cross-domain edges deferred to P5-11. Specialized epistemology (social/virtue/formal/skepticism varieties) deferred to P5-01b. End state: P5-01a complete in auto_target.json; check_target.py --task-id P5-01a passes both criteria; backlog count unchanged unless in-band findings warrant Issues.
outcome_summary: First Phase 5 routine-mode session against T-PHASE-5. Authored 0011_seed_epistemology_part1.sql with 28 nodes + 34 edges covering the analysis-of-knowledge tradition core (foundational + analysis spine + Gettier-and-responses + theories of justification + knowledge-dependent peripheral). All 28 nodes INTERPRETED (100%, well above the 70% floor and below the 20% SYNTHETIC ceiling at 0%). All 34 edges pedagogical_prerequisite, no cycles. graph_version 1 -> 2. Migration applied via Supabase MCP apply_migration; validate.py with venv Python runs 30 checks, 0 hard-fails, 27 soft-warns (25 expected missing_rigor_score per phase_5.md T2-C; 2 carryover issue_collision from MemPalace #1/#2). Work product committed at d8c4056 and pushed to main. Then: check_target.py --task-id P5-01a reported migration_applied FAIL; root cause is a one-line bug at engine/tools/check_target.py:114 (queries WHERE version = %s but version is the timestamp; auto_target.json id is the migration name). Fix is outside P5-01a's scope_lock so routine-mode posture filed Issue #9 (bug, priority:urgent), marked P5-01a blocked: scope-expansion-needed, wrote HANDOFF entry, and is closing without unblocking. Backlog 2 -> 3 (#1, #2 upstream + new #9). Routine doesn't need explicit pausing for safety because every other Phase 5 subject task has P5-01a in its depends_on and eligibility requires depends_on entries to be complete (P5-01a is now blocked, not complete) — _select_task returns no eligible task on next fire. The S-0051 interactive session lands the column-swap fix, marks P5-01a complete, and the routine resumes.

scope_delivery.explanation: Work product (the migration itself, ROUTING narrative entry, validator-clean state) landed exactly as declared. The criterion-pass bookkeeping (mark P5-01a complete) blocked on a verifier bug discovered post-apply: check_target.py:_check_migration_applied queries WHERE version = %s but the auto_target.json id is the migration name (version is the timestamp). Fix is outside P5-01a's scope_lock, so routine-mode posture treats this as scope-expansion-needed: filed Issue #9, marked P5-01a blocked, wrote HANDOFF, and exited cleanly. The 'declared scope' end state explicitly named 'P5-01a complete' as a deliverable; that flag flipped to 'blocked' instead of 'complete'. Recording delivered=false honestly. The work itself is in main; the contract-bookkeeping deferral is the gap. S-0051 interactive session lands the one-line fix and flips P5-01a to complete.
